description: Non-monotonic display name progression: Level 9 maps to 'excellent' but Level 10 maps to 'mediocre'. Quality names go UP from 1-9, then DROP sharply at 10 before climbing again 11-20. A player training from Level 9 to Level 10 would appear to get worse.
suggestedFix: Reorder the display names so they form a strictly monotonic progression from weakest (Level 1) to strongest (Level 20) using official Hattrick terminology.

description: GetDisplayName performs a direct dictionary lookup with DisplayNames[level]. If called with a SkillLevel value not in the dictionary (e.g., (SkillLevel)0), it throws an unhandled KeyNotFoundException.
suggestedFix: Use TryGetValue with a guard clause, or throw a descriptive ArgumentOutOfRangeException.

description: SkillLevel default value (0) is not a valid enum member. default(SkillLevel) is 0, which does not correspond to any defined member. Uninitialized fields silently hold an undefined value.
suggestedFix: Add a sentinel value like None = 0 or Undefined = 0, or start the enum at 0.

description: Test name GetDisplayName_Progression_IsMonotonic claims monotonicity but only checks string equality against hardcoded values. The test passes despite the non-monotonic semantic progression.
SkillType enum lacks explicit integer values, unlike every other enum in this codebase. If serialized to JSON save files, adding/removing/reordering members later will silently break deserialization.
suggestedFix: Add explicit values: Keeper = 0, Defending = 1, Playmaking = 2, etc.
